summary: Spanish Baroque painter from Seville, known as the 'Spanish Caravaggio' for his use of chiaroscuro/tenebrism. Known for religious paintings including The Martyrdom of Saint Serapion, Agnus Dei (a bound lamb), and Still Life with Lemons, Oranges, and Rose. Painted many works for the Carthusians and the Hall of Realms (Buen Retiro Palace). Contemporary of Diego Velazquez.

works.name: The Martyrdom of Saint Serapion (1628)
works.description: White-robed Mercedarian saint with arms bound to a tree by ropes, head slumping to the right. Massive white scapular contrasts with dark background. A small scroll reading 'B. Serapius' at right. Uses chiaroscuro.

clues.clue: White-robed saint with arms bound to a tree by ropes, head slumping to the right
examples: The title saint wears a badge of the Mercedarians and slumps his head to the right as he dies with both of his arms tied to a tree
a man in a flowing white robe whose two hands are held in ropes

clues.clue: Massive white scapular contrasts with dark background; red and yellow (or orange) Mercedarian badge pinned to white robes; evidence he has not yet been disemboweled or decapitated
examples: painted a red and yellow Mercedarian badge on the stark white robes of a different deceased saint
One artist from this country showed an orange crest pinned to the white robe of a martyred saint

clues.clue: Small scroll reading 'B. Serapius' at right
Uses chiaroscuro
examples: This artist used chiaroscuro to depict the title white-robed figure

works.name: Agnus Dei (Lamb of God)
works.description: A pure white lamb lying on a dark field with its feet/legs bound together. Stark chiaroscuro.

clues.clue: A lamb lying with its feet/legs tied together on a field of black and gray
examples: a pure white lamb with its feet bound
a lamb with its legs tied up

clues.clue: Stark tenebrism/chiaroscuro

works.name: Still Life with Lemons, Oranges, and Rose
works.description: Best-known genre painting. A plate of lemons, a basket of oranges, and a cup of water with a rose in its saucer, spaced at equal intervals. May have been intended as a homage to the Virgin Mary.

clues.clue: Plate of lemons, basket of oranges, cup of water with a rose in its saucer, spaced at equal intervals
examples: a plate full of lemons, a basket of oranges, and a cup of water with a rose in its saucer are spaced at equal intervals

clues.clue: May have been intended as a homage to the Virgin Mary

images.caption: Still Life with Lemons, Oranges, and Rose

works.name: Works for the Hall of Realms (Buen Retiro Palace)
works.description: Painted The Defense of Cadiz against the English and a cycle on the Labors of Hercules for the Salon de Reinos. These hung alongside Velazquez's The Surrender of Breda.

clues.clue: The Defense of Cadiz against the English — Duke of Fernandian gestures from armchair as sea battle takes place; shared theme with play by Rodrigo de Herrera
examples: The gout-afflicted Duke of Fernandian gestures at his generals from an armchair

clues.clue: Labors of Hercules — 10 panels including Hercules about to bring club down on the Nemean Lion
Works hung in the Salon de Reinos alongside Velazquez's The Surrender of Breda
St. Hugh in the Refectory — seven men clad in white saying grace, unaware of St. Hugh entering; elderly title character instructs young novice (painted for the Carthusians)
examples: The elderly title character instructs a young novice in the ways of his religious order
depicted seven men clad in white saying grace, unaware of St. Hugh entering the refectory

clues.clue: Bruno of Cologne witnessing meat turning into ash before Carthusian monks
Christ Crucified — unusually shows four nails rather than three
Saint Francis 'in meditation' — hooded monk with ripped robe clutching upside-down skull to chest
Apotheosis of Saint Thomas Aquinas — 1631 altarpiece; Charles V kneels below Thomas in clouds; does not use tenebrism; Seville college commission
examples: A group including Charles V kneels below this man in the clouds

clues.clue: Immaculate Conception — stairway, mirror, and single star appear in gaps of cloud cover; moved from tenebrism to brilliantly golden version; Pacheco codified iconography of teenage girl on crescent moon
examples: A stairway, a mirror, and a single star appear in the gaps of the cloud cover in a painting of this subject by Francisco de Zurbaran

Virgin Mary descending from pink-clouded heaven with putti heads emerging from bottom of robe; also depicted Mary and Jesus in Nazareth
Still-lives influenced by Juan Sanchez Cotan's bodegon genre; bodegones are Spanish paintings set in kitchens/taverns or still-lifes of food

examples: Francisco de Zurbaran's early depictions of this object feature a pin at the bottom; in a later Zurbaran depiction, this object has a strikingly blotchy reddish-ochre image

comprehensive_summary:
Francisco de Zurbarán (1598–1664) was a Spanish Baroque painter from Seville, known as the 'Spanish Caravaggio' for his heavy use of chiaroscuro and tenebrism. His paintings can be divided into 'cool,' 'warm,' and 'vaporous' styles depending on the degree of tenebrism used.

His most frequently clued work is The Martyrdom of Saint Serapion, depicting a white-robed Mercedarian saint with arms bound to a tree by ropes, head slumping to the right. A red and yellow (or orange) Mercedarian badge is pinned to the stark white scapular, which contrasts with the dark background. A small scroll reading 'B. Serapius' appears at right. Agnus Dei depicts a pure white lamb lying with its feet tied together on a dark field — a painting that doubles as both a bodegon and a religious painting.

His Still Life with Lemons, Oranges, and Rose is his best-known genre painting, showing a plate of lemons, a basket of oranges, and a cup of water with a rose in its saucer, spaced at equal intervals — possibly intended as a homage to the Virgin Mary. His still-lifes were influenced by Juan Sanchez Cotan's bodegon genre (Spanish paintings set in kitchens/taverns or featuring food).

For the Hall of Realms in the Buen Retiro Palace, Zurbarán painted The Defense of Cadiz against the English (showing the gout-afflicted Duke of Fernandian in an armchair as a sea battle rages) and a cycle of ten panels on the Labors of Hercules. These hung alongside Velazquez's The Surrender of Breda in the Salon de Reinos.

Other notable works include St. Hugh in the Refectory (seven men clad in white saying grace, unaware of St. Hugh entering) and Bruno of Cologne witnessing meat turning to ash (painted for the Carthusians), Christ Crucified (unusually with four nails rather than three), paintings of Saint Francis 'in meditation' (hooded monk clutching an upside-down skull), the Apotheosis of Saint Thomas Aquinas (a 1631 altarpiece showing Charles V kneeling below Thomas, unusually not using tenebrism), the Immaculate Conception (stairway, mirror, and single star in gaps of cloud cover; moved from tenebrism to brilliantly golden versions), The Death of St. Bonaventure (clergy around deceased saint on rich orange cloth), and the Veil of Veronica (early versions with a pin at the bottom, later with blotchy reddish-ochre image). He also led a 'cabal' with Battistello Caracciolo and Belisario Corenzio that monopolized commissions in Italy. He was a contemporary of Diego Velazquez and influenced the early career of Bartolome Esteban Murillo.
recursive_suggestions: Caravaggio — Zurbarán known as the 'Spanish Caravaggio'
Velázquez — contemporary, their works hung together in the Hall of Realms
Murillo — based early style on Zurbarán's works
